name: Power BI REST API
what_it_does: Microsoft Power BI REST API — programmatic access to Power BI service for managing reports, datasets, dashboards, and workspaces, enabling agents to automate dataset refreshes, embed analytics, and manage Power BI content in enterprise Microsoft environments.
use_cases: Agents triggering Power BI dataset refreshes after data pipeline completion to keep reports current
Embedded analytics — agents generating Power BI embed tokens for embedding interactive reports in enterprise applications
Workspace automation — agents creating and managing Power BI workspaces, datasets, and reports programmatically
Report distribution — agents exporting Power BI report pages as PDF/PNG for scheduled email distribution
Dataset management — agents pushing streaming data to Power BI streaming datasets for near-real-time dashboards
not_for: Non-Microsoft environments — Power BI is deeply integrated with Azure AD; heavy friction without Microsoft 365 ecosystem
Open-source BI — Power BI requires Microsoft licensing; use Metabase or Apache Superset for open-source alternatives
Public-facing dashboards — Power BI Pro is required for external sharing; use Grafana for public metrics dashboards
best_when: You're in a Microsoft 365 ecosystem and need to automate Power BI administration, dataset refreshes, or embed analytics in internal applications.
avoid_when: You're not using Azure AD/Microsoft 365 — Power BI's auth and licensing are too heavily Microsoft-dependent to use effectively in non-Microsoft environments.

auth.notes: Azure Active Directory OAuth2 required. Service principal (app registration) for unattended agent access — recommended over user credentials. Scopes include Dataset.ReadWrite.All, Report.ReadWrite.All, Workspace.ReadWrite.All. Tenant ID required in auth URL. Admin APIs require Power BI Administrator role.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Power BI Free: personal use only, no sharing or collaboration features
pricing.paid_tiers: Power BI Pro: $10/user/month; Premium Per User: $20/user/month; Premium Per Capacity: from $4,995/month
pricing.notes: Most API features require Power BI Pro license. Embedding requires Premium capacity or Premium Per User for external users. Often included in Microsoft 365 E5 and some E3 plans. API calls themselves are not separately charged.

performance.rate_limits: Dataset refresh: 8 per day per dataset (Pro); Workspace: no hard limits documented; Admin API: 200 req/hour

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Microsoft-standard REST API documentation. Error responses follow OData error format. PowerShell module available. Azure AD auth complexity is the primary agent friction. Good documentation but auth setup is involved.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Dataset refresh triggers are idempotent in concept but limited to 8/day. Report/dataset creation is not idempotent — duplicate names cause errors. Workspace creation with same name fails if workspace exists.
known_agent_gotchas: Azure AD app registration requires admin consent for org-wide service principal access — cannot self-authorize in most enterprise tenants
Dataset refresh limit (8/day for Pro) is a hard constraint — agents must track refresh count and implement scheduling logic
Service principal must be enabled in Power BI tenant settings by Power BI administrator before API access works
Power BI Premium capacity required for API-based embed tokens for external users — Pro-only limits external embedding
OAuth tokens expire — agents must implement Azure AD token refresh using client credentials flow for unattended operation

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure AD OAuth2 with granular scopes. FedRAMP authorized. HIPAA BAA available. ISO 27001 / SOC2. GDPR with EU data residency. Microsoft's enterprise security posture is excellent. Service principal auth with least-privilege scopes is recommended.
